Squid game- started watching season 2 the day it came out before I watched season one oops but immediately loved it. If you love a lil gore and DARK themes u will love this and a recommendation, if you don’t speak Korean plssss js turn subtitles on. With the English audio u lose all the amazing acting 
Wednesday- watched the whole thing, bored me to death. 
Stranger things- started watching when s4 came out and it’s my whole personality now. Like if u like gore and sci fi and comedy u will love love love this. Cast is soso sweet too

comment_text:
Squid game twitter leaks! 
Episode 1:the games continue
Description: After a failed mission Gi-hun gets taught a lesson. The next voting begins and the players get ready for the next game, Meanwhile Jun-ho is still in search for the mysterious island. Kang No-eul bends the rules of the game
Episode 2:the one with the key
Description: The game begins and tension is high. The group is further divided, seperated into teams, friendships are broken and betrayals unfold, Jun-Ho learns the truth about one of his partners, creating a problem.
Episode 3:a rainy day
Description: The players return as the VIP's are welcomed, giving Jun-Ho an opportunity, however suspicion's are raised.
Episode 4:cheol-su
Description not leaked yet :(
Episode 5:one last game
Description: Jun-ho finally finds a way into the games with some help, he blends in - and adjusts to the environment. The Front Man discovers a problem before continuing with the games.
Episode 6:squid game
Description: Before the Final game begins Gi-Hun finds out the truth about a former allie, will Jun-Ho be able to stop the games before its too late?
Hope this helps!

comment_text: I've been thinking of dropping Netflix for hulu, but the Wednesday series may be the primary reason that I haven't yet, though Squid Game and Stranger Things sure do help me to keep it, as well as One Piece.  It's just that in the past year I've found so little in the way of new shows that I really enjoy watching on Netflix, but many of the streaming services have become that way.  I think too many streaming services have diluted the market to where it becomes more difficult to raise money for new projects, of course with the advertising that they've all added, you'd think that would have helped them along.
